name: osc-score-birmingham

env_axis.name: original-vs-replication
env_axis.shift_mechanism: replication shift = sample + analyst + analysis-method differences combined; field varies

ood_protocol.name: replication-pair
ood_protocol.ref: Open Science Collaboration 2015 Science (RPP); Camerer et al. 2018 NHB (SSRP); Birmingham Replication DB

facts: OSC RPP 100 (psychology, 2015), DARPA SCORE ~3000 (social sciences), Birmingham Replication DB 1,051+; original-vs-replication pairs across multiple fields.
hypotheses: Original-vs-replication is a compound shift (sample + analyst + analysis-method combined), not a single shift type.
The same replication method does not produce field-invariant success rates: some fields are categorically more replicable than others.
intuitions: 2024-25 replication meta-analyses likely exist that pool these archives.
ML4Science / SCORE follow-up studies probably extend these results.
An AI-prediction-of-replicability literature likely exists that predates the SCORE results.
unknowns: 'Replication' itself is contested — exact, conceptual, and systematic replications produce categorically different shift mechanisms but are often pooled with no separating label.
The archive presupposes a single-shot pair as the unit; many-lab replications carry different signal but are rarer, and the pair cardinality needed for stable estimates cannot be determined.
epistemic_state.boundary: The pair unit aligns perfectly (study-pair = original + replication), but pair outcomes carry label noise atypical of other paired datasets; this is the corpus's only meta-science entry.

materialization_status: minimal OSF download (9.5 KB RPP CSV) — incomplete. Full archive (OSC RPP 100 + SCORE ~3000 + Birmingham 1,051+) needs proper OSF API access. Uploaded what was retrieved.
